title: Black-hole complementarity & its consistency
statement: Black-hole complementarity (Susskind, Thorlacius, Uglum 1993; 't Hooft) proposes that the descriptions seen by an infalling observer (who passes a smooth horizon) and an external observer (who sees information thermalized and re-emitted on a 'stretched horizon') are complementary, not contradictory, because no single observer can access both. It was the leading reconciliation of unitarity with a smooth horizon until the firewall argument (#06) challenged its consistency. Whether complementarity is internally consistent, and how it is realized, is open.

originators.name: Gerard 't Hooft
originators.note: independent precursor ideas on horizon information and the holographic S-matrix.

ranking.rationale: A specific proposed mechanism (not a brute mystery), now partly superseded/challenged by firewalls and reframed by holography; ranked mid-list as an actively-debated consistency question rather than a deep open void.

key_ideas: No-cloning is respected because no observer sees information both inside and in the radiation.
The 'stretched horizon' is a fictitious membrane (a Planck length outside the true horizon) that absorbs, thermalizes and re-radiates information for the external observer.
Complementarity underlies and motivated the holographic principle.
Hayden-Preskill: a black hole that has already radiated half its entropy returns infalling information very quickly (the black hole as a near-optimal scrambler).
The firewall argument (#06) claims complementarity is inconsistent for old black holes; ER=EPR (#08) is a proposed rescue.
AdS/CFT gives a manifestly unitary external description, sharpening the question of how the interior is encoded.

approaches.name: Stretched-horizon membrane
approaches.summary: Model the horizon as a dissipative membrane for external observers (the membrane paradigm made quantum).

approaches.name: Holographic encoding (AdS/CFT)
approaches.summary: The interior is encoded in boundary CFT data; subregion/entanglement-wedge reconstruction.

approaches.name: Fast scrambling / Hayden-Preskill
approaches.summary: Quantify how quickly information is returned, constraining when complementarity must hold.

papers.title: The stretched horizon and black hole complementarity
papers.note: The paper that introduced black-hole complementarity and the stretched-horizon picture. Phys. Rev. D 48, 3743 (1993). Three-postulate framework reconciling unitarity for the distant observer with smoothness for the infalling observer.

papers.title: The world as a hologram
papers.note: Susskind's implementation of 't Hooft's holographic principle; J. Math. Phys. 36, 6377 (1995). Foundational for the holographic underpinning of complementarity.

papers.title: Dimensional reduction in quantum gravity
papers.note: The original holographic-principle proposal (Salamfestschrift, 1993; gr-qc/9310026). 't Hooft argued the degrees of freedom in a region scale with boundary area, motivating horizon-information ideas behind complementarity. Known classic, not surfaced by the search engine in original form.

papers.title: Particle creation by black holes
papers.note: Commun. Math. Phys. 43, 199 (1975). The thermal-emission result that created the information paradox complementarity tries to resolve. Canonical landmark cited from certain knowledge.

papers.title: Black holes and entropy
papers.note: Phys. Rev. D 7, 2333 (1973). Horizon entropy proportional to area — the counting later interpreted holographically. Canonical landmark cited from certain knowledge.

papers.title: Information in the holographic universe / Average entropy of a subsystem (Information in black hole radiation)
papers.note: Page, 'Information in black hole radiation', Phys. Rev. Lett. 71, 3743 (1993); hep-th/9306083. Introduced the Page time and Page curve central to any unitarity-restoring scenario. Canonical landmark cited from certain knowledge.

papers.title: Black holes as mirrors: quantum information in random subsystems (Hayden-Preskill)
papers.note: JHEP 0709:120 (2007); arXiv:0708.4025. The 'black hole as a mirror' thought experiment: information dumped into an old black hole returns rapidly if the dynamics scramble. Canonical landmark cited from certain knowledge (search surfaced derivative follow-ups, not the original).

papers.title: Fast Scramblers
papers.note: JHEP 0810:065 (2008); arXiv:0808.2096. The fast-scrambling conjecture: black holes scramble in time ~ (beta/2pi) log S, the fastest in nature. Canonical landmark cited from certain knowledge.

papers.title: Towards the fast scrambling conjecture
papers.note: JHEP 04 (2013) 022. Lieb-Robinson lower bound on scrambling time plus model examples; key technical support for the fast-scrambler conjecture.

papers.title: Black holes: complementarity or firewalls? (AMPS)
papers.note: JHEP 02 (2013) 062. The firewall argument: the three complementarity-friendly postulates are mutually inconsistent for an old black hole. The central modern challenge to naive complementarity.

papers.title: An apologia for firewalls (AMPSS)
papers.note: JHEP 09 (2013) 018. Sharpens the firewall argument and rebuts proposed escapes, including identifying interior with exterior degrees of freedom.

papers.title: Gauge-gravity duality and the black hole interior
papers.note: Phys. Rev. Lett. 111, 171301 (2013). Entanglement-independent firewall argument for typical states in AdS/CFT; addresses ER=EPR.

papers.title: Comments on black holes I: the possibility of complementarity (fuzzball complementarity)
papers.note: JHEP 01 (2014) 034. Response to AMPS proposing 'fuzzball complementarity' as a string-theory alternative; statistical identity of infalling measurements outside the horizon.

papers.title: Cool horizons for entangled black holes (ER=EPR)
papers.note: Fortschr. Phys. 61, 781 (2013). The ER=EPR conjecture: entanglement is geometrized as Einstein-Rosen bridges, proposed to defuse the firewall and reconcile complementarity.

papers.title: The Large-N Limit of Superconformal Field Theories and Supergravity (AdS/CFT)
papers.note: Adv. Theor. Math. Phys. 2, 231 (1998). The holographic duality that gives a concrete, manifestly unitary boundary description of a gravitating bulk — the strongest evidence that black-hole evolution is unitary, underpinning complementarity.

papers.title: Quantum computation vs. firewalls (Harlow-Hayden)
papers.note: JHEP 06 (2013) 085; arXiv:1301.4504. Argues that distilling the entanglement needed to detect a firewall is computationally intractable (exponential in the entropy), suggesting complementarity is protected by computational complexity. Canonical landmark cited from certain knowledge; search surfaced follow-ups (Python's Lunch, Weinstein review) rather than the original.

papers.title: A bound on chaos (Maldacena-Shenker-Stanford)
papers.note: JHEP 08 (2016) 106; arXiv:1503.01409. Universal bound lambda_L <= 2 pi / beta on the Lyapunov/OTOC growth rate, saturated by black holes — the precise sense in which black holes are maximal scramblers. Canonical landmark cited from certain knowledge (search returned derivative OTOC papers).

papers.title: Black holes and the butterfly effect
papers.note: JHEP 03 (2014) 067; arXiv:1306.0622. Shock-wave/OTOC analysis showing horizon dynamics scramble perturbations exponentially fast — the gravitational mechanism behind fast scrambling. Canonical landmark cited from certain knowledge.

papers.title: Efficient decoding for the Hayden-Preskill protocol
papers.note: arXiv:1710.03363. Explicit (probabilistic and Grover-based) decoding algorithms recovering information from Hawking radiation, tying scrambling/OTOC decay to information recovery.

papers.title: The Page curve of Hawking radiation from semiclassical geometry (islands)
papers.note: JHEP 03 (2020) 149. Introduces the island rule: the radiation's entanglement wedge includes the black-hole interior, yielding a unitary Page curve. (Consensus listed grouped AHMSZ authorship; primary authors Almheiri-Mahajan-Maldacena-Zhao.)

papers.title: Entanglement wedge reconstruction and the information paradox
papers.note: JHEP 09 (2020) 002. Quantum-extremal-surface phase transition at the Page time gives the Page curve and recovers Hayden-Preskill via entanglement-wedge reconstruction, without a firewall.

papers.title: Replica wormholes and the entropy of Hawking radiation
papers.note: JHEP 05 (2020) 013. New gravitational-path-integral saddles (replica wormholes) reproduce the island rule and the unitary Page curve in JT gravity.

papers.title: The Python's Lunch: geometric obstructions to decoding Hawking radiation
papers.note: JHEP 08 (2020) 121. Geometrizes Harlow-Hayden computational hardness as a wormhole obstruction (the 'Python's Lunch') between non-minimal quantum extremal surfaces.

papers.title: The ghost in the radiation: robust encodings of the black hole interior
papers.note: JHEP 06 (2020) 031. Pseudorandomness + quantum error correction protect the interior from computationally bounded observers, sharpening the complexity-based defense of complementarity.

papers.title: The entropy of Hawking radiation (review)
papers.note: Rev. Mod. Phys. 93, 035002 (2021). The definitive recent review of fine-grained gravitational entropy, islands, and the unitary Page curve for Hawking radiation.

papers.title: Complementarity for a dynamical black hole
papers.note: Phys. Rev. D (2024). Recent reformulation arguing the interior lies in the causal past of the radiation, so no operation on the radiation can affect an infalling observer — a modern defense of complementarity using the stretched horizon.

physicists.name: Leonard Susskind
physicists.contribution: Co-proposed black-hole complementarity and the stretched horizon, the holographic principle ('world as a hologram'), the fast-scrambler conjecture, and ER=EPR.

physicists.name: Gerard 't Hooft
physicists.contribution: Pioneered the horizon S-matrix / brick-wall view of black-hole information and originated the holographic principle (dimensional reduction in quantum gravity).

physicists.name: John Preskill
physicists.contribution: Co-authored the Hayden-Preskill 'black holes as mirrors' thought experiment and quantum-information analyses of the interior encoding.

physicists.name: Patrick Hayden
physicists.contribution: Co-authored Hayden-Preskill scrambling/information-mirror result and the Harlow-Hayden computational-complexity defense of complementarity.

physicists.name: Joseph Polchinski
physicists.contribution: Co-author of the AMPS firewall argument and its sharpenings, the central challenge to naive complementarity. (Deceased 2018.)

physicists.name: Ahmed Almheiri
physicists.contribution: Lead author of the AMPS firewall papers and of the island-rule / Page-curve and Hawking-radiation-entropy program.

physicists.name: Juan Maldacena
physicists.contribution: Discovered AdS/CFT (the unitary boundary dual underpinning complementarity), co-proposed ER=EPR, and proved the universal bound on chaos with Shenker and Stanford.

physicists.name: Don N. Page
physicists.contribution: Introduced the Page time and Page curve — the benchmark any unitarity-restoring (complementarity-compatible) scenario must reproduce.

physicists.name: Geoffrey Penington
physicists.contribution: Showed entanglement-wedge reconstruction with quantum extremal surfaces yields the Page curve and recovers Hayden-Preskill without a firewall; co-author of the Python's Lunch.

physicists.name: Douglas Stanford
physicists.contribution: Co-derived the chaos bound and the black-hole butterfly-effect/shock-wave picture of fast scrambling, and contributed to replica-wormhole computations.

simulator_angle: The classical membrane paradigm (a conductive horizon membrane) is a visualization the simulator could render; the quantum complementarity question is beyond it.

metadata.coverage_note: Assembled 26 genuinely-relevant papers and 10 physicists for Problem #7 (black-hole complementarity and its consistency). Method: ~11 Consensus paper-search MCP calls across varied sub-queries (stretched horizon/complementarity, Hayden-Preskill scrambling, fast scramblers, holographic principle, firewalls/AMPS, ER=EPR, AdS/CFT, islands/Page curve, replica wormholes, entanglement-wedge reconstruction, Harlow-Hayden complexity, RMP review). 17 papers were directly surfaced by search and carry real Consensus URLs (plus arXiv/DOI where known); 9 are canonical landmarks I cite from certain knowledge with bibliographic notes and arXiv/DOI identifiers but no search URL: 't Hooft 1993 (gr-qc/9310026), Hawking 1975, Bekenstein 1973, Page 1993, Hayden-Preskill 2007 (0708.4025), Sekino-Susskind 'Fast Scramblers' 2008 (0808.2096), Harlow-Hayden 2013 (1301.4504), Maldacena-Shenker-Stanford 'A bound on chaos' (1503.01409), and Shenker-Stanford 'Black holes and the butterfly effect' (1306.0622). The list count is 26 (one over the 25 target); if a strict cap is needed, the most droppable is Shenker-Stanford 'Black holes and the butterfly effect' since the chaos bound (MSS) and fast-scrambler entries already cover that theme. Tiers: this is an OPEN problem, so most entries are open_conjecture; Hawking 1975 and Bekenstein 1973 are analytic_classical (exact/semiclassical GR). No observational-constraint papers were included because complementarity is not currently observationally testable. Honesty note: a few search hits returned grouped/abbreviated author lists (e.g. the 2019 Page-curve paper and the 2024 'Complementarity for a dynamical black hole'); I supplied the correct primary authorship where I am confident and flagged the 2024 paper's secondary authors as 'et al.' rather than inventing names. No titles, years, arXiv ids, DOIs, or URLs were fabricated.
